title: D0-A vector case contracts (normative companion, D-91)
description: Companion to d0a-vector.v1.json (the container schema, spec §13.1). A vector is valid only if it validates against BOTH schemas; a harness never invents family semantics. This file closes the per-family case_kind vocabulary and pins, per case_kind, the exact `inputs` and `expected.result` field schemas. Object inputs are lowercase hex of canonical CBOR bytes; results are canonical encodings or closed-enum strings; `outcome`/`disposition` strings are cross-validated by the harness against spec §10.4/§10.5 (no duplicated enum to drift). Authored before any fixture (D-91). In scope for the Gate-A prose↔vector discrepancy audit: behavior a fixture needs that this file cannot express folds back into the specification first. Authored against spec v0.5.19, SHA-256 410880e04433b629d5d11956e322f59832494d8f25042b3dfcf34d8b694c6748.

opening-tranche: family 11 journal-replay: deferral schedules (erase request vs nonterminal journal; retrieval exclusion immediate; adopted-erasure residual) — D-198
family 7 fold: C1→I→C2 vs C1→C2→I delayed-reference convergence (unheld ref pends, admits on arrival) — D-199
family 11 journal-replay: statement-kind XferReopen.basis fails at parse; statement-kind invalidation verifies — D-193/D-200
family 13 journal-replay: Abort/Reopen and competing terminals inside one Txn — (frame ordinal, record index) order — D-200
family 7 fold: pending c.revoke_device → grant issued in the window → completing continuation (cessation = completing carrier) — D-195
family 7 fold: staged frontier → revoke last grant → regrant (the frontier consumed the stage; no resurrection) — D-196
family 11 fold: frozen owner → collision loser → freeze-basis death → loser re-enters the claimant fold — D-196
family 7 fold: negation-residual acceptance (P enrolled/burned first; candidate −P accepted under a named role and device) — D-190
notes: case_kind names are contract names; the scenario lives in `name` and `source`.
Contracts shared across families dispatch on case_kind alone; the family tier closes which kinds each family may use.
`converge: true` obligates the harness to run EVERY listed delivery order PLUS a fresh fold of the union and assert identical final state (the three-run standard).

then.$comment: The sign-then-verify lane draws P-256 signing nonces from the vector RNG, and WebCrypto's ECDSA cannot inject them (Ed25519 signing is deterministic — the constraint is P-256's, review R8.10): browsers get verify-fixed, never signing draws.

hexcbor.$comment: lowercase hex of canonical CBOR bytes (E1–E10)

items_map.$comment: named canonical operation/statement/record bytes referenced by deliveries and results

per_item_result.$comment: FINAL per-item classification — arrival-invariant, one list for all deliveries: exactly one row per delivered item; a row WITHOUT the (outcome, disposition) pair asserts the item finally ADMITS (is in effect) — E10 pins the closed enums to failures, so admission carries no failure pair (same optional-classification design as journal-replay per_record)

state_probes.$comment: named canonical-encoding probes of derived state (frontier hash, admit_bound value, owner, …); probe names cite the spec construct they encode

trace.$comment: optional INTERMEDIATE assertions: in delivery #d, immediately after `after` folds, `item` holds (outcome, disposition)

aux.$comment: non-delivered context bytes (installed descriptors, policies) — never folded, only resolved

evidence_class.$comment: amendment #7 (the ff23f1cd review's F1): the evidence-lifecycle lane's machine-readable arrival-structure declaration — which qualified evidence (late or timely) is held at the ORIGINAL operation's first evaluation in every listed delivery. Required on evidence-lifecycle vectors (the case-kind contract pins it); meaningless elsewhere. Within one declared class the listed deliveries must derive identical final verdicts on every relative delivery order of original and re-proposal (D-204 + D-205); convergence across classes is not promised (the D-204 residual).

sizes.$comment: encoder-exact byte sizes (the pinned worst-width figures, joint fits)

inputs.$comment: the CLOSED §2 tag inventory — a new tag is a spec change, never a fixture change
key_id = H_key({alg, pk}) (alg REQUIRED by the harness for kind=key_id); mat_id = H_mat(SEC1 point bytes) (alg absent)
sk absent ⇒ key material comes from rng draws (draw_order names it)
delivery defaults to items in the listed order; subject defaults to the last delivered item — the op whose (outcome, disposition) is asserted

expected.$comment: Amendment 3 (repair tranche): the negative arm — a checksum-invalid mnemonic is rejected BEFORE derivation (spec 2.4), so a phrase-derive case may carry an (outcome, disposition) pair instead of derived keys.

inputs.$comment: semantic journal folds supply items+deliveries; byte-level replays supply frames (+ crash_cuts as byte offsets into the concatenated stream)

then.$comment: the construct-and-rederive contract (D-127/D-156): fixtures start from sources + keys; the harness independently re-derives the root, paths, and release_op

released.$comment: amendment #5 (review R4): the reducer's DERIVED release verdict over the independent read-release input — completeness (indexes exactly 0..count-1), disjointness, exact union with the released result_ids, and one-Txn membership; REQUIRED whenever inputs.release is present

rotation_ops.$comment: Amendment #4 (D6): the signed c.kek_rotate operations the control plane accepted, as canonical triple bytes. Every durable Fence must resolve its rotation through Fence.rotation_op = H_op over one of these entries (the hash covers the signature bytes); state-5 tombstone re-derivation and durable-tombstone content validation bind to the resolved op's typed erase_manifest — never to unsigned stream content.

then.$comment: family 14: the umbrella RFC App C #2 offline confirmation — the run's result is recorded in spec §15 when performed

arrival_is_semantic.$comment: amendment #6 (review R7, the D-202 ruling): evidence-arrival order is this lane's SEMANTIC INPUT — an issued lease-stale is terminal where issued and later timely evidence revives nothing; per-replica divergence on the original op is ruled, convergence rides the re-proposed op. The listed deliveries share one declared arrival structure and must agree; no fresh-fold or generated-order convergence applies.

aux.$comment: non-delivered context bytes (installed descriptors, policies) — never folded, only resolved

release.$comment: amendment #5 (review R4): the INDEPENDENT read-release event — the result ids released to the caller and the audit rows (by item name) that rode the read's one Txn; the reducer derives `released` from held rows against these, never from the vector's own chunk table
